summary: Iter 15 verified. Backend host-request flow: POST /api/host-requests creates pending row + host_request broadcast notification for super_admin; second call returns same pending; 60s auto-approve promotes viewer to host and emits host_granted notification. Frontend: 'Create a room' CTA (create-room-cta) visible in Public rooms with role-differentiated caption. Non-host clicks fire POST /api/host-requests, open request-host-dialog showing exactly 'Requested to SuperAdmin for hosting. You'll be notified the moment it's approved.' — no '60 seconds' or 'auto-approve' copy. Header 'New watch room' button behaves identically (auto-fires on dialog open). Super-admin clicking the CTA opens the create-room dialog and does NOT fire host-requests. Static code review of WatchRoom.js confirms playKnockSound: feature-detected (window.AudioContext || webkitAudioContext), try/catch wrapped, AudioContext cached in knockAudioRef.current, resumed if suspended, invoked from join_request WS handler (line 157). Iter 7 knock-flow tests already validated join_request WS message delivery.

test_results.frontend_host_cta_visible_and_caption: PASS ('Spin up a new public room')
test_results.watchroom_playKnockSound_static_review: PASS (feature detect + try/catch + cached ctx + resume + wired to join_request)

critical_code_review_comments: Backend POST /api/host-requests response body still includes `auto_approve_at`. Spec says frontend already ignores it, but if you want fully user-invisible timing, consider removing this field from the API response too.
seed_data_creation: Ephemeral iter15_hr_* viewers created + cleaned per-test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 15 complete: host-request flow, public-create-room-btn CTA, and playKnockSound wiring all verified. Doorknock audio was verified statically per instructions (Playwright can't capture Web Audio output).
